c74object.name: doubles~
c74object.digest: Vocal alignment using DTW and WSOLA
c74object.description: The doubles~ object aligns the timing of a subject vocal buffer to match a reference vocal buffer. It uses Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) for feature extraction, Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) for finding the optimal alignment path, and Waveform Similarity Overlap-Add (WSOLA) for artifact-free time-stretching.

inlet.description: Accepts the 'align' message to trigger the offline alignment process.

outlet.description: Outputs progress as a float (0.0-1.0), followed by a 'finished [buffer]' message and a bang when complete.
Inflection points for the 'function' object. Sends 'clear' followed by individual 'x y' list pairs.

method.name: reference
method.description: Sets the name of the buffer to be used as the timing reference for alignment.

method.name: subject
method.description: Sets the name of the buffer containing the audio that will be warped to match the reference.

method.name: destination
method.description: Sets the name of the buffer where the aligned audio will be written.

method.name: align
method.description:
Triggers the background alignment process using the buffers previously specified with the reference, subject, and destination messages. Only the subject buffer is required (set via the subject message); if the destination buffer is unset, it defaults to the subject buffer. If the reference buffer is unset, the object attempts to find a matching reference in the 'stems' dictionary by looking for another buffer with a matching normalized filename (ignoring timestamps and '(doubles)' tags).
The destination buffer will be resized and cleared to match the reference buffer's length if its current length is different. If the destination buffer is already the correct length, it will not be cleared, allowing data from previous alignment operations with different spans to be retained. If the subject is stereo, the destination will be processed as stereo, applying the same timing warp to both channels. Inflection points of the alignment path are output from the second outlet for visualization in a 'function' object.
Optional Arguments:
• start_ms: The start time in milliseconds for the alignment span.
• end_ms: The end time in milliseconds for the alignment span.
If a span is specified, the alignment is only performed on that portion of the buffers. The destination buffer will match the full reference buffer length, with the areas outside the aligned span either remaining as they were (if the buffer was already the correct length) or filled with silence (if the buffer was resized).

method.name: undo
method.description: Restores the destination buffer to its state prior to the most recent align operation. This includes reverting any resizing that occurred during that operation. Only one level of undo is supported.

method.name: export
method.description:
Writes the contents of the last used destination buffer to disk. The export location is constructed as: [project_path]/Samples/Processed/Consolidate/[filename].
The filename is determined by looking up the destination buffer name in a dict object named 'stems'. The retrieved filename is modified by inserting " (doubles) " before the timestamp and updating the timestamp to the current time in [YYYY-MM-DD HHMMSS] format.

attribute.name: log
attribute.description: Enable or disable detailed diagnostic logging to the third outlet (Index 2). When enabled, the object will post status updates regarding buffer settings and alignment progress.

attribute.name: targetbias
attribute.description:
Sets the strength of the "gravity" pulling the alignment towards the ideal DTW position during the synthesis (WSOLA) phase.
When warping audio, the algorithm searches a small range around the target position to find the best waveform match. In silent or quiet regions, waveform correlation can be unreliable. The target bias provides a penalty for candidates far from the target, preventing "drift" in gaps.
Suggested Scale:
• 0.0: No bias. Pure waveform similarity based on normalized cross-correlation.
• 0.01 (Default): Subtle timing guide. Recommended for most vocal applications to maintain stability in silent gaps.
• 0.05 - 0.1: Moderate pull. Useful if the alignment feels like it's "wandering" or if the vocal performance is very loose.
• 0.5 - 1.0: Heavy pull. Prioritizes rigid timing over waveform phase-matching (use for rhythmic speech or percussive elements).
